seoTitle: Moondyne vs Moonies Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Moondyne and Moonies Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Moondyne vs Moonies Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Moondyne and Moonies Hill.
heading: Moondyne vs Moonies Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Moondyne, Western Australia and Moonies Hill, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Moonies Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Moondyne (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Moondyne or Moonies Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Moondyne has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Moonies Hill scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Moondyne scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Moonies Hill scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Moondyne or Moonies Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Moonies Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
